content.full_text:
December 3, 1992

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
General Counsel  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
425 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Stan Oken  
Fresno County Supervisor

Dear Mr. Hallabrin,

The purpose of this letter is to request a ruling on the status of a gift received by a local officeholder from a former mother-in-law.

Mr. Oken, a Fresno County Supervisor, is a 50% beneficiary in the will of his former mother-in-law. Mr. Oken has maintained a familial relationship with her for more than 30 years. She moved to her present home specifically to be closer to him and he has cared for her since her move. The former mother-in-law has offered to gift $50,000 to Mr. Oken immediately, which will be subtracted from his interest in her estate.

For purposes of reporting gifts on Form 721, and for purposes of the limits on gifts in Government Code §89501, is this gift an exempt gift either,

a) as coming from his former mother-in-law, or

b) as being an inheritance or bequest?

Please respond in writing as soon as possible. Thank you in advance for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Gary A. Colbert, CPA  
Asst. Treasurer  
Committee To Re-Elect Stan Oken
